reviews.review_msg: Enjoyed class but grading criteria was very unclear, especially on writing assignments which made it really difficult to respond to feedback/improve grade on the next assignment.

Here are the things about Michelle Walks

- She has good inspiring lectures

- But her making of grading along with her trustworthy T.A. are strict

- Her participation in discussing is merely strict as well

-

Natasha was great in giving great feedbacks to her students, and was a very easy person to approach as she is very passionate and caring person. Th readings are not dense, and quite very long so please be aware of that. The more you get in the class discussion the more your absorb the information. A lot of group discussion which is great!

Th readings are not dense, and quite very long so please be aware of that. The more you get in the class discussion the more your absorb the information. A lot of group discussion which is great!

Th readings are not dense, and quite very long so please be aware of that. The more you get in the class discussion the more your absorb the information. A lot of group discussion which is great!

Best prof i've had so far. Connects and explains course concepts and readings so well. Readings were tough but explained during class. Participation matters, he'll call on you to answer questions. Discussions in class were very insightful. I thought i'd hate the class but it ended up being my fav. Wouldn't suggest it if you don't like anth tho.

Hands down one of my fave professors. Assigns heavy but interesting readings and will call on you so participation matters. But he is judgement free and super chill, will never make you feel stupid. He gives great feedback and wants you to be the best!! He is super approachable as well. Would totally take again :) I hope he stays!
Avoid him if you are not interested in the topic. His classes are not easy.

Elliot is a great and passionate professor that encourages class participation and discussions. Readings are mandatory as he will call on you, but overall its a safe space and an enjoyable class. I definitely recommend him
Great professor and provides amazing feedback, he really cares for your success. The only bad thing is the long and sometimes boring lectures.

Professor does not provide clear instructions for key graded activities. When reaching out the feedback is poor, and fails to answer or clarify the question being asked.

took two in person courses and one online course with this professor, laid back but caring, very knowledgeable and easy to talk with. I had difficulty with my term paper, but a zoom chat with her solved all my concerns.

I thought Jie Yang was an amazing professor. I think what makes her seem inaccessible to some if that she is an incredibly intelligent person. Tough weekly assignment. But, she is a fair grader and an understanding, compassionate person. She has the most calming voice. Generally, the easiest way to go about this course is to listen to her lectures.

Jie was okay. She wasn't really clear on what she wanted from the students at the beginning, and you were only able to figure it out if you saw her in office hours. Even then, I was still unsure of what she wanted. The final exam was terrible. It was basically us having to search through all the readings and group presentations and lecture notes.
Professor does not provide clear instructions for key graded activities. When reaching out the feedback is poor, and fails to answer or clarify the question being asked.

not a fair grader or good at teaching how to write for a heavy extensive writing class. Feedback is never helpful because it's always 'this is not correct' rather than explaining where you went wrong. Very critical and harsh at grading written assignments for an easy 2nd year class. The course and readings are easy but her grading is not awful.

Assignments are straightforward; they are short writing assignments but there are quite a few of them for the semester. She gives awesome feedback on written work, more than what you think. My only issue with the course, was how the lecture material was presented - it was little dry. But other than that, it was a fair course.
Has a great ability to let the class struggle with a question and lead the lecture. Extremely knowledgable.
so boring, her lectures are hard to listen to and it doesnt even matter cause its all common sense and theres no exams. if you show up and put minimal effort you will be able to do well.

Pam is a professor I had to learn to love. Her marking scheme was often unclear and you wouldn't find out what she was looking for till you got your work back. We did multiple exercises to improve our writing skills and I really appreciate her for that. The textbook used was by her but it's a great collection of ethnographies that were made easy.

Yeah don't take her class, you will buy the textbook she wrote so good luck with presenting your own ideas. TA seemed more up to date than professor, grading is very brutal and there is not much emphasis on helping students become better writers in the course, bad office hrsAlso speaking in her class is not very well received, skip this one folks.

Stern replies to people's opinions with either a "uh okay" or a "you're wrong" yet expect all of us to participate  Reading assignments due almost every week with unclear instructions and unfair grades Not sure why she's still at sfu

I thought I enjoyed her, only to be disappointed later in the semester. She's a hard marker and this particular class requires a lot of participation (it's a small class). The instructions on her reading responses also aren't the clearest.

Tough marker. Very fickle. Strange way of grading assignments. Didn't feel I learnt too much. Would have liked to use the text and the theories in there a lot more. Strict with guidelines and what she requires in her assignments. Will give you advise, but doesn't come off that approachable. High interest in the subject. Decent seminar arrangement.

Not a good experience with his online class. The TA did all the marking, and John didn't even show up for any of the exams. I understand large classes are busy, especially a W class, but I was discouraged by the lack of participation by the prof. The TA had unclear expectations. I wouldn't take a class with him again if this is how he conducts them

John is a very fair, well-meaning, and passionate guy. Lots of films in this class, probably wishes we would participate more & he deserves that tbh. Lectures are basically like extended tutorials- you have to teach yourself the lecture every week with 60pages from his self-written learning module (free!) & extra readings. Better keep up.
